2604.12632.title: Calibration-Aware Policy Optimization for Reasoning LLMs
2604.12632.paper_summary: Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) enhances LLM reasoning but often induces overconfidence, where incorrect responses yield lower perplexity than correct ones, degrading relative calibration as described by the Area Under the Curve (AUC). Existing approaches either yield limited improvements in calibration or sacrifice gains in reasoning accuracy. We first prove that this degradation in GRPO-style algorithms stems from their uncertainty-agnostic advantage estimation, which inevitably misaligns optimization gradients with calibration. This leads to improved accuracy at the expense of degraded calibration. We then propose Calibration-Aware Policy Optimization (CAPO). It adopts a logistic AUC surrogate loss that is theoretically consistent and admits regret bound, enabling uncertainty-aware advantage estimation. By further incorporating a noise masking mechanism, CAPO achieves stable learning dynamics that jointly optimize calibration and accuracy. Experiments on multiple mathematical reasoning benchmarks show that CAPO-1.5B significantly improves calibration by up to 15% while achieving accuracy comparable to or better than GRPO, and further boosts accuracy on downstream inference-time scaling tasks by up to 5%. Moreover, when allowed to abstain under low-confidence conditions, CAPO achieves a Pareto-optimal precision-coverage trade-off, highlighting its practical value for hallucination mitigation.
2604.12632.paper_summary_zh: 群體相對政策優化（GRPO）增強了大型語言模型（LLM）的推理能力，但常常導致過度自信，錯誤的回應所產生的困惑度低於正確的回應，這降低了相對校準，如曲線下面積（AUC）所描述的那樣。現有的方法要麼在校準上僅有有限的改進，要麼犧牲推理準確性的增益。我們首先證明，GRPO風格算法中的這種退化源於其對不確定性無關的優勢估計，這不可避免地使優化梯度與校準不對齊。這導致了在準確性提高的同時校準退化。我們接著提出了校準感知政策優化（CAPO）。它採用理論上一致且允許後悔界限的邏輯AUC替代損失，實現了對不確定性敏感的優勢估計。通過進一步結合噪聲掩蔽機制，CAPO實現了穩定的學習動態，聯合優化校準和準確性。在多個數學推理基準上的實驗顯示，CAPO-1.5B顯著提高了校準，最多達到15%，同時達到與GRPO相當或更好的準確性，並在下游推理時間擴展任務中進一步提高準確性，最多達到5%。此外，當在低信心條件下被允許放棄時，CAPO實現了帕累托最優的精確度-覆蓋率權衡，突顯了其在減輕幻覺方面的實際價值。
2604.12632.author: Ziqi Wang et.al.
2604.12632.authors: Ziqi Wang, Xingzhou Lou, Meiqi Wu, Zhengqi Wen, Junge Zhang
